finding: Homepage credibility counters render as "0+", "$0M+", "0+" in the captured frame, so the site's strongest proof block reads as literally zero at a glance.

evidence.quote:
0+
ENTREPRENEURS LAUNCHED
$0M+
SNACK/DRINK SALES IN VENDINGPRENEUR MACHINES
0+
VENDING LOCATIONS W/OUR GUIDANCE

persona_rationale: I scan a homepage for proof in seconds. Stats that read 0+ and $0M+ are worse than no stats at all — they tell me the program has launched nobody and sold nothing.

suggested_fix.current: Animated count-up counters captured at their 0 start state: "0+", "$0M+", "0+"
suggested_fix.proposed: Render the final values server-side (e.g. "500+", "$8M+", "1,200+") so they are correct before any animation, or disable the count-up so the real numbers show immediately above the fold

finding: Homepage above-the-fold renders at a cramped, downscaled width compared to other pages (header nav squeezed) and has a high layout shift.
persona_rationale: A confident first impression matters in my 30 seconds; a squeezed, shifting hero reads as unpolished before I've absorbed the message.

suggested_fix.current: Hero/header renders narrow with CLS 0.263 on load
suggested_fix.proposed: Reserve space for hero image and partner logos to eliminate the shift, and ensure the header lays out at full viewport width on first paint

finding: Contact is absent from the header navigation; the only paths are the footer link or knowing the URL.

evidence.quote:
ABOUT
RESOURCES
CASE STUDIES
NEWS
STEP INSIDE

persona_rationale: When I want to reach someone I glance at the top nav. If Contact isn't there I assume there's no easy way to reach the team and I move on.

suggested_fix.current: Header nav: About, Resources, Case Studies, News (no Contact)
suggested_fix.proposed: Add a "Contact" item to the primary header navigation

finding: Application submit success is shown only as a small inline green line of text next to the button, with no banner, no redirect, and the form left fully populated.

evidence.quote: Thanks. We received your details and will follow up shortly.

persona_rationale: I glance after clicking Submit. If the form still looks full and there's no prominent confirmation, I conclude it failed and either resubmit or leave annoyed.

suggested_fix.current: Inline green text "Thanks. We received your details and will follow up shortly." beside an unchanged form
suggested_fix.proposed: Redirect to the existing /thank-you-for-applying page (or show a full-width success banner and clear/disable the form) on successful submit

finding: Above-the-fold article cards show blank/broken thumbnails — one card renders raw alt text instead of an image — caused by a 500 image error, making first paint look half-loaded.
persona_rationale: A broken thumbnail at the top of the news page tells me in a glance the site isn't maintained — that erodes trust before I read a word.

suggested_fix.current: Article image returns 500 from /_next/image; card shows raw alt text
suggested_fix.proposed: Fix or re-host the failing CDN image and add a styled fallback thumbnail so a broken image never shows raw alt text

finding: The news listing page heading is "BLOG" while the nav item and route are "NEWS".
persona_rationale: I clicked NEWS and landed on a page titled BLOG; that mismatch makes me second-guess whether I'm where I meant to be.

suggested_fix.current: Nav/route "News" lands on a page headed "BLOG"
suggested_fix.proposed: Use one consistent label — either rename the heading to "News" or rename the nav item and route to "Blog"

finding: Horizontal scroll present at mobile width.
persona_rationale: I often open links on a phone in a meeting; horizontal scroll signals a layout that wasn't finished for mobile.

suggested_fix.proposed: Constrain the case-study card row to the viewport width (overflow handled within a contained carousel, not the page)

finding: A clear, well-designed application confirmation page exists but the apply form never routes to it; the real flow instead shows buried inline text.

evidence.quote: A dedicated /thank-you-for-applying page EXISTS in the app but the apply form does not route to it.

persona_rationale: The good confirmation I'd want already exists — leaving it orphaned while the live flow hides the success signal is the easiest high-value fix on the site.

suggested_fix.current: Apply form shows inline green text; /thank-you-for-applying is never reached

finding: The Readiness and Status columns are shown as bare colored dots (red/blue/amber) with no visible legend, so a color's meaning is only available on hover per row.
persona_rationale: I want to scan the table and instantly see which pages are in trouble. Colored dots with no key force me to hover every row — the opposite of glanceable.

suggested_fix.current: Readiness/Status rendered as unlabeled colored dots; meaning only in hover/aria text
suggested_fix.proposed: Add a short legend above the table and/or text labels beside the dots (e.g. "Blocked", "Needs work", "Ready")

finding: A "Schedule failed" state is exposed as a filter but is not summarized in the top KPI cards, so a failure isn't visible at a glance.
persona_rationale: If something is failing I want it in the headline cards, not hidden behind a filter chip I have to think to click.

suggested_fix.current: "Schedule failed" only appears as a workflow filter link
suggested_fix.proposed: Surface a count of schedule failures in the KPI card strip when greater than zero

finding: The Blog/news CMS has no entry in the studio sidebar; it is reachable only by direct URL or breadcrumb.

evidence.quote: NO News/Blog item in the sidebar — sidebar offers only "SEO pages", "Media library", and "Settings".

persona_rationale: I orient by scanning the sidebar. If News isn't listed there, I'd conclude the studio can't manage news at all.

suggested_fix.current: Sidebar: SEO pages, Media library, Settings (no News)
suggested_fix.proposed: Add a "Blog and news" item to the studio sidebar navigation

finding: On first open the page builder shows a "Quick Tour" overlay that covers the outline panel it points at and blocks interaction until dismissed.

evidence.quote: On FIRST open a "Quick Tour" overlay (Step 1 of 3) covers the outline panel and blocks interaction until dismissed

persona_rationale: An overlay that blocks the very panel I'm trying to use wastes the few seconds I have; I'll dismiss it blind and possibly miss what I needed.

suggested_fix.current: Quick Tour overlay covers the outline panel on first builder open
suggested_fix.proposed: Position the tour tooltip so it does not occlude the target panel, and allow interaction with the page while the tour is open

finding: The Redirects manager has no inbound links from any explored admin page — it is an orphaned route reachable only by URL.

evidence.quote: Redirects | NO PATH FOUND — /admin/pages/redirects is not linked from any explored admin page (zero links to it in all ARIA extracts)

persona_rationale: A functional feature I can't find by scanning the UI may as well not exist to me.

suggested_fix.current: No link to /admin/pages/redirects anywhere in admin nav
suggested_fix.proposed: Link Redirects from the SEO pages screen header or the studio sidebar

finding: The same content type is labeled four different ways across the app: route "News", public heading "BLOG", editor "Blog CMS"/"New blog post", and nav "NEWS".

evidence.quote: Naming is inconsistent across the same content type: sidebar-less section is "News" in the route, "Blog CMS"/"New blog post" in the editor, "NEWS" in public nav, "BLOG" as the public page heading.

persona_rationale: Four names for one thing forces me to re-learn the same concept at every screen — it slows the instant recognition I rely on.

suggested_fix.current: News / BLOG / Blog CMS / New blog post used interchangeably
suggested_fix.proposed: Pick one canonical label (e.g. "News") and apply it to the nav, route, public heading, and editor consistently
